claim: Agents SDK separates harness (orchestration, state management, secrets) from compute (sandbox) enabling ephemeral containers that snapshot and rehydrate without losing model continuity
actionable: Use separate clients for harness and sandbox in Agents SDK; define snapshot storage (R2, local tarball) and reuse across tasks via session management

claim: Async shell interaction (execute command, don't wait for completion, resume later) is a Codex-inspired pattern ported to Agents SDK enabling agents to manage multiple long-running processes
actionable: For long tasks, use async shell commands in Agents SDK rather than blocking; model can context-switch and resume processes as dependencies complete

claim: Manifest objects in Agents SDK unify file population strategies: copy files, mount S3/R2 buckets, mount GitHub repos, or mount volumes natively supported by sandbox provider
actionable: Use manifest entries to mount large datasets from cloud storage instead of copying to avoid startup delay; trade latency for freshness and scale

claim: Function tools in Agents SDK are Python/TypeScript functions decorated with @function_tool that auto-translate to API schema; SDK routes model calls automatically without manual orchestration
actionable: Wrap custom tools as decorated functions; avoid manual function_calling loops by relying on SDK routing

claim: Tool call approvals in Agents SDK prevent risky operations: decorate high-stakes tools with approval functions (static or dynamic) that block execution until human confirms
actionable: Set approval=True or approval=lambda(args): True for destructive/high-cost tools (database deletes, financial transactions) to add human-in-the-loop checkpoints

claim: Skills in Agents SDK are bundles (SKILL.md + scripts/resources) versioned in GitHub; agents SDK pulls from git repo with version control, enabling centralized skill libraries
actionable: Store skills in GitHub with git tags for versioning; reference skill_git_repo + branch in agent config; iterate skills via PR workflow

claim: Context compaction is a built-in capability allowing agents to summarize old messages and stay under context limits while working for hours/weeks without reset
actionable: Enable context compaction capability to allow agents to exceed initial context window and operate indefinitely without human intervention

claim: SDK-owned vs user-owned sandboxes: SDK-owned (default) spins up/down automatically; user-owned persists across turns allowing warm reuse and multi-turn sessions
actionable: For one-off tasks use SDK-owned; for interactive development or multi-turn workflows create user-owned sandboxes and manage lifecycle explicitly

claim: Harnesses optimize for model distributions; Codex harness and Agents SDK harness are different but cross-model compatible, allowing flexibility vs best performance tradeoff
actionable: If using OpenAI models, use the native harness for best performance; if multi-provider, expect performance degradation but broader compatibility via Agents SDK

claim: First-class support for modal, EDB, Cloudflare, Vercel, Daytona, Blackel containers; can also bring custom sandbox client by inheriting BaseSandboxClient
actionable: Choose a sandbox provider you already use (modal volumes for snapshots, Cloudflare R2 for files); if none match, implement BaseSandboxClient
